$comment: Canonical Nostr wire contract between ndisc (publisher) and the glmps web viewers (consumers). v2 supersedes v1 — see schema/README.md and schema/v2-proposal.md. Source of truth: xjmzx/ndisc src-tauri/src/lib.rs (release_event / delete_release); the contract test `mod schema_v2` pins ndisc's output to this file.

release.content: Release notes — plain text. May be an empty string.

d.note: The ONLY structurally guaranteed tag. The `disco-vault:` prefix is the legacy app name and is frozen — do not change it to `ndisc:`.

medium.note: OPTIONAL — frequently absent. Consumers must not require it.

tracks.note: ADDITIVE (2026-06): expected total tracks for the release, integer rendered as a string. A release property (from the source TRACKTOTAL metadata), NOT a count of what's present on any one device. Optional and backward-compatible — older consumers ignore it; emitted only when known. Does not bump the schema version.

discs.note: ADDITIVE (2026-06): total number of discs in the release, integer rendered as a string. A release property like `tracks`, NOT a per-device count. Derived from the release's format breakdown (e.g. Discogs format quantities, so 2x LP counts as 2 discs), so it is present only for releases enriched from such a source — digital folder imports carry no disc count. Emitted only when greater than zero; consumers surface it only for genuine multi-disc releases (> 1). Optional and backward-compatible — older consumers ignore it. Does not bump the schema version.

condition.note: Discogs grade verbatim, e.g. 'Near Mint (NM or M-)'.

source.note: URL — emitted only when it is http(s).

genre.note: NEW IN v2. Up to three ordered genre slots: first = primary, second = secondary, third = tertiary. Each slug is one of the active values in `genreSlugs` (35 after the 2026-06 restructure; the four deprecated compound pairs remain valid for legacy reads). Order of `genre` tags in the emitted event IS the priority order — emitters MUST preserve it, consumers MUST treat it as authoritative. Invariants enforced by the emitter and validated by conformant consumers (see `genreInvariants`).

release.rule: Every tag except `d` is omitted entirely when its value is empty. Consumers MUST require only `d` structurally and treat all other tags — including `title`, `artist`, `medium`, and `genre` — as optional.

a.note: Addressable coordinate of the deleted release. Authoritative — match this verbatim.

k.note: Redundant: the kind is already encoded in `a`. Consumers may ignore `k`.

deletion.rule: Consumers MUST apply deletions client-side. Some relays (e.g. Primal) do not enforce kind:5, so server-side filtering cannot be relied on.

genreSlugs._comment: Grouped for semantics + palette ONLY — NOT a hierarchy; all active slugs are pure peers (see genreInvariants). The groups map to three colour tiers: `acoustic` (primary, muted/earthy), `electronic` (secondary, vivid), `tertiary` (optional/cross-cutting). `bridge` slugs (dub, noise) sit between the acoustic and electronic families and carry standalone hues. A zero-count slug renders no chip in data but stays selectable in pickers. Adding a slug is additive (re-pin the SHA, no v3 bump) provided existing slugs stay stable; renaming or removing a slug is a v3 bump. Display is verbatim except `rnb` → 'R&B' and the glmps-side cosmetic `soundtrack` → 'film'.

deprecated._comment: Compound slash-pairs from the original v2 set, retired in the 2026-06 restructure when they were split/collapsed into atomic slugs. NEVER emitted on new events; retained as VALID for reading legacy events and cross-relay NIP-77 copies, where they still display with a slash. Mapping: classical-folk→classical+folk, dnb-jungle→dnb+jungle, drone-noise→noise, footwork-trap→footwork.

genreInvariants._comment: Cross-slot constraints. The emitter enforces these at capture time; conformant consumers SHOULD validate on read with strict-but-recoverable handling (silently drop the offending slot, keep the rest). All 35 active slugs are pure peers — the acoustic / electronic / bridge / tertiary groups are palette + semantic groupings, not a hierarchy; any combination across groups is permitted in the same event. Meaning composes by stacking slugs rather than implying parent semantics.
genreInvariants.distinct: All emitted `genre` slugs in a single event MUST be distinct.
genreInvariants.denseOrdering: Slots are dense on the wire — present slugs occupy the lowest indices. A consumer receiving a sparse sequence (e.g. via a non-canonical re-emitter) SHOULD compact before indexing.

validation.unknownGenreSlug: Treat as absent (no colour, no chip) OR render with a neutral fallback. Do NOT throw or fail the parse.
validation.invariantViolation: Silently drop the offending slot(s); keep the remaining valid ones. Do NOT throw.

profile.note: ndisc reads the author's kind:0 metadata (name / nip05 / picture) for display. It never publishes kind:0.

changePolicy: FROZEN once shipped, with two convention-permitted ADDITIVE exceptions that do NOT force a v3 bump (the `d`-only structural rule guarantees old consumers stay compatible): (1) new optional slugs inside `genreSlugs`, existing slugs stable; (2) new OPTIONAL tags that consumers may ignore — e.g. the additive `tracks` and `discs` tags (2026-06). Any BREAKING change — renaming/removing slugs or tags, changing tag semantics, breaking ordering, or making a previously-optional tag required — is a coordinated v3 bump: add release.v3.json and migrate consumers, never edit this file. When an additive amendment lands, re-vendor this file into the glmps + ndisc.view consumers and re-pin their SHA.
rolloutFromV1: v1 readers ignore the `genre` tag (additive rule) and surface no genre data until upgraded. ndisc emits only v2 format; no v1-compatible proxy (e.g. NIP-12 `t` tags) is added. See schema/v2-proposal-glmps-reply.md for the v2-only emission decision rationale.
